commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Matrix crypto bootstrap sequencing so forced cross-signing reset bootstraps secret storage before cross-signing, disables the forced-reset repair retry, and updates adjacent unit tests.

PR surface: Source -6, Tests +6. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still skips initial SSSS bootstrap for forced reset, allows a repair-block SSSS recreation, and can call setupNewCrossSigning again. I did not run a live Matrix homeserver reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs a contributor or owner update for the Matrix E2EE state blocker plus live behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not advance a mock-only external PR automatically.

target.title: fix(matrix): prevent double bootstrapCrossSigning reset in --force-reset-cross-signing
target.body:
## Summary

- Bug: `--force-reset-cross-signing` triggers `bootstrapCrossSigning` twice. The first call is deferred by `deferSecretStorageBootstrapUntilAfterCrossSigning`, which skips the initial SSSS bootstrap. When cross-signing reset breaks SSSS, the repair block inside `bootstrapCrossSigning` detects broken SSSS and calls `resetCrossSigning` a second time, destroying E2EE state. The recovery key file is left pointing at an empty secret storage, rendering E2EE unrecoverable.
- Root cause: The `deferSecretStorageBootstrapUntilAfterCrossSigning` conditional path in `bootstrap()` was designed to handle password-UIA scenarios but created a gap where SSSS was not repaired before the destructive reset, leading to a cascading double reset via the SSSS repair block.
- Fix: Always bootstrap SSSS before cross-signing. For forced reset, run SSSS bootstrap with `strict: false` and `allowSecretStorageRecreateWithoutRecoveryKey: true` so broken SSSS is repaired upfront. Pass `allowSecretStorageRecreateWithoutRecoveryKey: false` to `bootstrapCrossSigning` during forced reset so its internal repair block does not trigger a second cross-signing reset. Remove the old `deferSecretStorageBootstrapUntilAfterCrossSigning` branch entirely.
- Fixes #78396

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-bootstrap.test.ts` — 21 passed (1 test file)
- Test `"recreates secret storage upfront and does a single forced cross-signing reset"` — verifies single `bootstrapCrossSigning` call, two `bootstrapSecretStorageWithRecoveryKey` calls (upfront + post-cross-signing key pickup)
- Test `"re-exports cross-signing keys after forced reset creates secret storage"` — confirms SSSS is bootstrapped twice (upfront + key pickup) but cross-signing only once
- Test `"does not bootstrap SSSS upfront when cross-signing key upload requires UIA"` — updated to expect upfront SSSS bootstrap even for UIA case, which is safe (SSSS recreation is no-op when healthy) and strictly better than double-reset
- Autoreview: clean
- CI: pending

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `--force-reset-cross-signing` no longer destroys E2EE state by calling `bootstrapCrossSigning` twice. The recovery key file now points at a valid secret storage after forced reset.

Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22, branch `fix/issue-78396-matrix-crypto-double-reset`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm test extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-bootstrap.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  21 passed (21)
   Start at  22:53:22
   Duration  3.94s (transform 1.71s, setup 384ms, import 3.04s, tests 161ms, environment 0ms)
```

Observed result after fix: All 21 tests pass. The three forced-reset tests (`"recreates secret storage upfront and does a single forced cross-signing reset"`, `"re-exports cross-signing keys after forced reset creates secret storage"`, `"trusts the fresh own identity after a forced cross-signing reset"`) confirm `bootstrapCrossSigning` is called exactly once, verifying the double-reset is eliminated.

What was not tested: Live Matrix homeserver E2EE environment. The fix is covered by comprehensive unit tests that mock the Matrix SDK crypto API at the correct boundary.
target.author: xialonglee
